question_text: A sealed container has a fixed volume of 1 m³. When the gas inside is heated from 300 K to 600 K, the pressure increases from 100 kPa to 200 kPa. Explain why the pressure doubled even though the volume remained constant.
correct_answer: When temperature doubles, the gas particles move faster and collide with the container walls more frequently and with greater force. This increases pressure. At constant volume, pressure is directly proportional to absolute temperature (Gay-Lussac's Law).
explanation: According to kinetic theory, heating a gas increases particle speed, leading to more forceful and frequent collisions with container walls. Since the volume is fixed, this directly increases pressure proportionally to the absolute temperature.
